<a id="t-742644861d"></a>
## Thread: MF compatible compiler for Red Hat Linux 9. — 2 msgs

_Topics: [`Compilers and vendors`](topics.md#compilers)_

### Re: MF compatible compiler for Red Hat Linux 9.

- **From:** hhinman <member30421@dbforums.com>
- **Date:** 2003-10-15T14:45:01-04:00
- **Newsgroups:** comp.lang.cobol
- **Message-ID:** `<3485519.1066243501@dbforums.com>`
- **References:** `<3402673.1064675308@dbforums.com>`

```

Here is a response from Fujitsu regarding their new Linux COBOL
compiler:



Does anyone know of a cobol compiler that does the following:

(a) Runs on Red Hat Linux 8.0 and 9?

              7.2, 7.3 and is being certified for 8.0, 9.0, Advance
                Server 2.1





(b) Compiles MF Cobol II & COBOL-85 source codes without much change



              Generally speaking, that is true.  You need to know that
              the Linux version of our compiler  does not support the
              screen section.  If you are creating a work station
              application, you need to use a product like Flexus SP2 for
              the user interface.





(c) Creates both statically and dynamically linked executables



              Yes.
```

#### ↳ Re: MF compatible compiler for Red Hat Linux 9.

- **From:** riplin@Azonic.co.nz (Richard)
- **Date:** 2003-10-15T19:37:17-07:00
- **Newsgroups:** comp.lang.cobol
- **Message-ID:** `<217e491a.0310151837.59c9e820@posting.google.com>`
- **References:** `<3402673.1064675308@dbforums.com> <3485519.1066243501@dbforums.com>`

```
hhinman <member30421@dbforums.com> wrote

> Here is a response from Fujitsu regarding their new Linux COBOL
> compiler:

It's not particularly new, I've had it for a year or so.
 
> Does anyone know of a cobol compiler that does the following:
> 
> (a) Runs on Red Hat Linux 8.0 and 9?
>               7.2, 7.3 and is being certified for 8.0, 9.0, Advance
>                 Server 2.1

It also does work on Mandrake 9.x.

> (b) Compiles MF Cobol II & COBOL-85 source codes without much change
>               Generally speaking, that is true.  You need to know that
…[3 more quoted lines elided]…
>               the user interface.

Though they say 'screen section' it is the whole MF ADIS module that
is not present.  You can use ANSI ACCEPT/DISPLAY but the whole ACCEPT
AT, FROM CRT, CRT-STATUS.

There are also no CBL_ or PC_ routines - though these are easily
replaced.

There are many minor differences, such as requiring an actual variable
to be defined for 'SELECT .. ASSIGN dynamic-name' which can be
implicit in MF.

There is no MF DE-EDIT"1", so I have had to change all these to
FUNCTION NUMVAL.

> (c) Creates both statically and dynamically linked executables
>               Yes.

Yes, that works fine, it creates .so.  It is case sensitive though, so
while I had all program names as lower case under MF I had to make
them upper case for Fujitsu.  Not really a problem though.
```

<a id="t-7016082be9"></a>
## Thread: Logic Bug — 15 msgs

### Logic Bug

- **From:** yukonmama@aol.com (YukonMama)
- **Date:** 2003-10-16T04:24:07+00:00
- **Newsgroups:** comp.lang.cobol
- **Message-ID:** `<20031016002407.23480.00000250@mb-m16.aol.com>`

```
This is probably obvious to you all as the pimple on a teenagers nose but I'm
having one of those days where nothing is making any sense.  Been staring at
this and just can't see the problem :)  I've tried to ask my compatriots but
they are duking it out over who did the most work on a particular project and
I'd rather avoid appearing to take sides :)

Below is a cut/past from the compile.  What is happening is the IF statement
following the PERFORM VARYING END-PERFORM is not being executed if the first IF
in the perform loop is true.  Since it appears I have the correct number of END
whatevers I'm at a loss as to why.  How do I know it isn't working?  There is a
calculation at the end of the program that uses the WS-KLIMA-CIR and
WS-NKLIMA-CIR which with the test data being used should be 4 and 6
respectively, they were 4 and 3.  Also there are 3 duplicates and they should
be in the WS-NKLIMA-CIR count.  I also had some DISPLAY statements in the IF
which I removed to be sure they weren't causing a problem (hey - check
everything) that didn't show when the first IF was true (indicated by it's own
display).  I executed the program without any DISPLAYS (as you see it below)
and the DISPLAYS at the end of the program giving the totals show that it still
didn't work.


           PERFORM VARYING SUBW FROM 1 BY 1       
                UNTIL SUBW > MAX-WIRES               
1                 IF (XXXX-WIRE-PREFIX OF BMCPP028 = 
1                       WIRE-PREFIX (SUBW)) AND      
1                    (XXXX-WIRE-BODY OF BMCPP028 =   
1                       WIRE-BODY (SUBW)) AND        
1                    (WIRE-GAUGE OF BMCPP028 =       
1                       WIRE-SUFFIX (SUBW))          
2                      COMPUTE SUBW = MAX-WIRES + 1  
1                 ELSE                                   
2                 IF WIRE-BODY (SUBW) = SPACES               
3                     MOVE XXXX-WIRE-PREFIX OF BMCPP028      
3                       TO WIRE-PREFIX (SUBW)                
3                     MOVE XXXX-WIRE-BODY OF BMCPP028        
3                       TO WIRE-BODY (SUBW)                  
3                     MOVE WIRE-GAUGE OF BMCPP028            
3                       TO WIRE-SUFFIX (SUBW)                
3                     IF WIRE-GAUGE OF BMCPP0    28 < '   12'    
4                         MOVE 'N' TO WIRE-KLIMA (SUBW)      
3                     ELSE                                   
4                     IF WIRE-GAUGE OF BMCPP028 > '   22'    
5                         MOVE 'N' TO WIRE-KLIMA (SUBW)      
4                     ELSE                                   
5                         MOVE 'Y' TO WIRE-KLIMA (SUBW)      
4                     END-IF                                 
3                     END-IF                        
3                     COMPUTE SUBW = MAX-WIRES + 1  
2                 END-IF                            
1                 END-IF                            
              END-PERFORM                           
                                                    
              IF WIRE-GAUGE OF BMCPP028 < '   12'   
1                 ADD 1 TO WS-NKLIMA-CIR            
              ELSE                                  
1             IF WIRE-GAUGE OF BMCPP028 > '      22'   
2                 ADD 1    TO WS-NKLIMA-CIR            
1             ELSE                                  
2                 MOVE 'Y' TO WS-KLIMA-SW           
2                 ADD 1 TO WS-KLIMA-CIR             
1             END-IF                                
              END-IF        
              .
```

#### ↳ Re: Logic Bug

- **From:** "William M. Klein" <wmklein@nospam.netcom.com>
- **Date:** 2003-10-16T06:42:44+00:00
- **Newsgroups:** comp.lang.cobol
- **Message-ID:** `<E5rjb.2653$7a4.2636@newsread4.news.pas.earthlink.net>`
- **References:** `<20031016002407.23480.00000250@mb-m16.aol.com>`

```
How are
      SUBW and  MAX-WIRES
defined?  Is there any chance that you are doing a "digit overflow" where
you have:
    COMPUTE SUBW = MAX-WIRES + 1

(Your symptoms don't "imply" this - but it is always a possibility if the
two fields are defined identically and Max-Wires is already at the maximum
size)
```

##### ↳ ↳ Re: Logic Bug

- **From:** yukonmama@aol.com (YukonMama)
- **Date:** 2003-10-17T02:54:16+00:00
- **Newsgroups:** comp.lang.cobol
- **Message-ID:** `<20031016225416.00343.00000863@mb-m27.aol.com>`
- **References:** `<E5rjb.2653$7a4.2636@newsread4.news.pas.earthlink.net>`

```

>From: "William M. Klein" wmklein@nospam.netcom.com 
>Date: 10/16/03 2:42 AM Eastern Daylight Time

>How are
>      SUBW and  MAX-WIRES
>defined?  Is there any chance that you are doing a "digit overflow" where
>you have:
>    COMPUTE SUBW = MAX-WIRES + 1

Both are PIC 9999 value 0. in working-storage.  Table has 50 occurances.
```

###### ↳ ↳ ↳ Re: Logic Bug

- **From:** "Ron" <NoNoSpam@StopSpam.org>
- **Date:** 2003-10-16T22:48:52-05:00
- **Newsgroups:** comp.lang.cobol
- **Message-ID:** `<mNqcnaA-Spg7-xKiXTWQlg@giganews.com>`
- **References:** `<E5rjb.2653$7a4.2636@newsread4.news.pas.earthlink.net> <20031016225416.00343.00000863@mb-m27.aol.com>`

```
What happens if you say:

             END-PERFORM

             DISPLAY 'I MADE IT HERE'.

              IF WIRE-GAUGE OF BMCPP028 < '   12'
                  ADD 1 TO WS-NKLIMA-CIR

It would at least confirm or deny whether you're exiting
the perform as expected.
If not, I'd also say 'END-PERFORM.' (with the period) to make
sure everything above is ended - at least to test.
If it still didn't work I'd get the assembly translation and see
what it is REALLY doing.
```

```
Hi YM,

I don't know about the IF after the END-PERFORM, but it looks like the
PERFORM will never execute if you donï¿½t change the initial value of
MAX-WIRES before the PERFORM is first executed. Do you?

If you don't, since SUBW  = 1 and MAX-WIRES = 0 upon entry, the UNTIL
SUBW > MAX-WIRES assures that the flow passes to the NSI following the
END-PERFORM on the 1st attempt to execute the PERFORM.

Regards, Jack.
```

###### ↳ ↳ ↳ Re: Logic Bug

- **From:** jacksleight@hotmail.com (Jack Sleight)
- **Date:** 2003-10-19T19:56:45-07:00
- **Newsgroups:** comp.lang.cobol
- **Message-ID:** `<8a2d426e.0310191856.1620fafb@posting.google.com>`
- **References:** `<8a2d426e.0310162201.1402a571@posting.google.com> <20031019004953.07836.00000414@mb-m16.aol.com>`

```
Hi YM,

The rule for initial entry into a PERFORM loop with TEST BEFORE
(default) is:

Set the identifier (SUBW) to the current FROM value [“+1“]
If Condition-1 [“SUBW > MAX-WIRES, i.e. +1 > 0”] is true, exit PERFORM
Else, execute specified set of stmts

You stated that MAX-WIRES is pic 9999 value 0 and SUBW is set to +1 by
the PERFORM stmt. With these values upon entry to the PERFORM the
PERFORM set of stmts is  NOT executed.

If “COMPUTE SUBW = MAX-WIRES + 1” is the only place in your pgm that
modifies MAX-WIRES, you’ll NEVER execute those PERFORM stmts because
SUBW [“+1”] is ALWAYS  > MAX-WIRES [“0“].

Regards, Jack.
  






yukonmama@aol.com (YukonMama) wrote in message news:<20031019004953.07836.00000414@mb-m16.aol.com>...
> >From: jacksleight@hotmail.com  (Jack Sleight)
> >Date: 10/17/03 2:01 AM Eastern Daylight Time
…[8 more quoted lines elided]…
> by SUBW which is reset by the varying clause.
```

```
PERFORM VARYING SUBW FROM 1 BY 1 - I do believe this sets SUBW to 1 no matter
what it was prior to the execution of the perform statement.   Now of course if
this doesn't and SUBW is always > MAX-WIRES then I never should have gotten to
the ELSE portion of the IF statement that was part of the PERFORM loop - which
I did.

It was the IF following the END-PERFORM that wasn't being executed - not the IF
within the perform loop.  This should have been executed no matter what SUBW
was - it wasn't controled by the PERFORM - it was a stand alone IF.
```

###### ↳ ↳ ↳ Re: Logic Bug

_(thread depth: 5)_

- **From:** jacksleight@hotmail.com (Jack Sleight)
- **Date:** 2003-10-25T22:29:03-07:00
- **Newsgroups:** comp.lang.cobol
- **Message-ID:** `<8a2d426e.0310252129.5b716752@posting.google.com>`
- **References:** `<8a2d426e.0310191856.1620fafb@posting.google.com> <20031023010234.04714.00001595@mb-m17.aol.com>`

```
Hi YM,

You said:

“... and SUBW is always > MAX-WIRES then I never should have gotten to
the ELSE portion of the IF statement that was part of the PERFORM loop
- which I did“.

And that’s my point: If MAX-WIRES is = to zero as pgm control
approaches the PERFORM (is this assumption correct?), then when the
PERFORM begins, before the 1st iteration of the loop, SUBW = 1 and
MAX-WIRES = 0. Which means that the UNTIL condition is met, and
therefore NO IF stmts in the PERFORM are executed and flow resumes at
the NSI after the END-PERFORM - “The IF That Didn’t Bark”. In fact,
the stmts in the PERFORM can NEVER execute (there is no iteration), if
as you say,  MAX-WIRES is a constant zero and doesn‘t change. Do you
agree? If not, why? It seems to me like they can’t.

But you say they ARE executed; you put a DISPLAY into the IF to prove
it and it displays! “Doo doo, doo doo ...”, Twilight Zone time. I hate
when this happens. :-)

BTW, if you didn’t write that code, someone may have used MAX-WIRES =
0 to effectively “comment out” that PERFORM. I also notice COMPUTEs
within the PERFORM that use MAX-WIRES. These will re-set SUBW to +1,
no matter what its current value is, if MAX-WIRES = 0.

It looks to me like you’ve got 2 problems, not one: An IF that
shouldn’t execute, but does, and an IF that should execute, but
doesn’t.

You didn’t say what environment you’re executing in, but I’m assuming
IBM mainframe. If so, you might want to recompile w/LIST,NOOFF
compiler options and take a look at the Assembler code generated. That
may give you a clue as to what the compiler thinks the code is doing.

I also like Ron’s suggestion of putting in a DISPLAY after the
END-PERFORM. In fact, I’d go him 1, maybe 10 or 15 better  (MY CODE
BEGINS W/===>), i.e.:

(To get rid of the DISPLAY stmts when no longer needed, in ISPF enter
on the cmd line: x all;f all debug-;del all nx)


===>        DISPLAY 'DEBUG- GOING INTO PERFORM' 
>           PERFORM VARYING SUBW FROM 1 BY 1       
>                UNTIL SUBW > MAX-WIRES
===>        DISPLAY 'DEBUG- IN PERFORM'                
>1                 IF (XXXX-WIRE-PREFIX OF BMCPP028 = 
>1                       WIRE-PREFIX (SUBW)) AND      
…[3 more quoted lines elided]…
>1                       WIRE-SUFFIX (SUBW))
===>        DISPLAY 'DEBUG- IN IF1'                
>2                      COMPUTE SUBW = MAX-WIRES + 1  
>1                 ELSE                                   
===>        DISPLAY 'DEBUG- IN ELSE1' 
>2                 IF WIRE-BODY (SUBW) = SPACES               
===>        DISPLAY 'DEBUG- IN IF2'
>3                     MOVE XXXX-WIRE-PREFIX OF BMCPP028      
>3                       TO WIRE-PREFIX (SUBW)                
…[4 more quoted lines elided]…
>3                     IF WIRE-GAUGE OF BMCPP0    28 < '   12'    
===>        DISPLAY 'DEBUG- IN IF3'
>4                         MOVE 'N' TO WIRE-KLIMA (SUBW)      
>3                     ELSE                                   
===>        DISPLAY 'DEBUG- IN ELSE3'
>4                     IF WIRE-GAUGE OF BMCPP028 > '   22'    
===>        DISPLAY 'DEBUG- IN IF4'
>5                         MOVE 'N' TO WIRE-KLIMA (SUBW)      
>4                     ELSE                                   
===>        DISPLAY 'DEBUG- IN ELSE4'
>5                         MOVE 'Y' TO WIRE-KLIMA (SUBW)      
>4                     END-IF                                 
…[4 more quoted lines elided]…
>              END-PERFORM                           
===>        DISPLAY 'DEBUG- OUT OF PERFORM' 
>                                                    
>              IF WIRE-GAUGE OF BMCPP028 < '   12'   
===>        DISPLAY 'DEBUG- IN IF5'
>1                 ADD 1 TO WS-NKLIMA-CIR            
>              ELSE                                  
===>        DISPLAY 'DEBUG- IN ELSE5'
>1             IF WIRE-GAUGE OF BMCPP028 > '      22'   
===>        DISPLAY 'DEBUG- IN IF6'
>2                 ADD 1    TO WS-NKLIMA-CIR            
>1             ELSE                                  
===>        DISPLAY 'DEBUG- IN ELSE6'
>2                 MOVE 'Y' TO WS-KLIMA-SW           
>2                 ADD 1 TO WS-KLIMA-CIR             
>1             END-IF                                
>              END-IF        
===>        DISPLAY 'DEBUG- END OF CODE'
>              .                 

That should cover the waterfront.
```

```
MAX-WIRES is a constant.  PIC 999 VALUE 50.  Doesn't change.  Therefore the
first time the PERFORM comes about SUBW is set to 1 and isn't greater than
MAX-WIRES which until someone changes the program will remain at 50.

Yes - the ELSE portion of the IF in the perform was executed.  I did put a
display in.  And the other side also was executed again verified by a DISPLAY.

PERFORM VARYING SUBW FROM 1 BY 1 UNTIL SUBW > MAX-WIRES   
      IF something
            DISPLAY 'THIS IS TRUE'
      ELSE something
             DISPLAY 'THIS IS FALSE'
      END-IF
END-PERFORM

Following this perform loop was this IF statement.  It is HERE I had the
problem...

IF something
     DISPLAY 'GOT IT'
ELSE
     DISPLAY 'DON'T HAVE IT'
END-IF

Now - if the 'THIS IS TRUE' portion of the IF in the the PERFORM loop was
executed then NONE of the displays in the 2nd IF showed up.  If the 'THIS IS
FALSE' portion of the IF in the PERFORM loop executed then I got one of the
other two displays.

Are we on the same page now?
```

###### ↳ ↳ ↳ Re: Logic Bug

_(thread depth: 7)_

- **From:** jacksleight@hotmail.com (Jack Sleight)

```
Hi YM,

Bill Klein asked:

"How are SUBW and MAX-WIRES defined?"  

You replied:

"Both are PIC 9999 value 0. in working-storage. Table has 50
occurances."

I took that to mean MAX-WIRES = 0. Now that you say that MAX-WIRES =
50, I think weâre on the same page.

It looks like the compiler is taking the IF following the PERFORM as
part of the ELSE clause of the 1st IF in the PERFORM. Why thatâs
happening is not clear. The IFs/END-IFs seem to match up. It doesnât
explain why you donât get a compiler error for the END-PERFORM in
the middle of an IF stmt. But then again, when a compiler gets
confused anything goes.

You can try this. I have no reason to offer why, except that they
occur in the 1st IF, but you might want to get rid of the parens
around each condition. What the hell, itâs worth a shot. It canât
do any harm. While youâre at it, you might want to put a DISPLAY
after the END-PERFORM to debunk or prove the âconfused compilerâ
hypothesis.

Regards, Jack.
```

###### ↳ ↳ ↳ Re: Logic Bug

_(thread depth: 9)_

- **From:** jacksleight@hotmail.com (Jack Sleight)
- **Date:** 2003-10-31T07:54:46-08:00
- **Newsgroups:** comp.lang.cobol
- **Message-ID:** `<8a2d426e.0310310754.3ac59da@posting.google.com>`
- **References:** `<8a2d426e.0310262112.48f5fd5c@posting.google.com> <20031030235647.19589.00000037@mb-m01.aol.com>`

```
Hi YM,

Just a cautionary note. I noticed a period after the last IF. I assume
that indicates an end of pgraph.

If you move the IF somewhere else, you've solved THAT problem, but
later someone else may add code after the PERFORM and run into a
similar problem. Granted, that's not YOUR problem :-) but just thought
I'd mention it.

My previous suggestion sounds like clutching at straws and it is, but
it's based on this premise:

Something in the code is causing it not to execute as expected or is
causing the compiler to misinterpret the code. So, eliminating pieces
of the code should eventually excise the offending segment (or
eliminate the code entirely :-).)

Since you can't eliminate required segments, you're limited to
optional segments. The parens are the only optional segments of the
code, sooo.

On another tack (I'm sure you would have done this, but JIC): does
your compiler support EVALUATE? You could combine IFs and EVALs. That
may circumvent the problem.

And another: I'm not sure if this was mentioned earlier, but
terminating the perform with a period will probably solve the problem.
But that's just disguising the problem, too.

Regards, Jack.

…[15 more quoted lines elided]…
> back and give this idea a try.
```

## Thread: Gotta Be A Better Way — 16 msgs

### Gotta Be A Better Way

- **From:** yukonmama@aol.com (YukonMama)
- **Date:** 2003-10-16T04:38:55+00:00
- **Newsgroups:** comp.lang.cobol
- **Message-ID:** `<20031016003855.23480.00000251@mb-m16.aol.com>`

```
Looking for ideas.  The bane of our shop is getting the mainframe and PC's to
talk to each other.  In my case I have a VSAM file on the mainframe (soon to be
a DB2 table) that needs mass updates at least once a month.  In some cases
these updates are downloaded from the web into a .CSV file which is then
manually edited by the user by removing some columns and adding others and then
manually uploading it to the mainframe via TSO $IN???.  The other is where I
have to extract data from another file and put it to a comma delimited file
which I then upload to my PC and e-mail to the user who then manipulates it and
does the TSO upload to the mainframe.

It never fails.  The user either messes up the data or messes up the upload. 
The job will abend at least 6 times before we get a good update.

There is no central place to FTP to or from which was one idea that someone
came up with. 

With all the experience etc here anyone got any good ideas?

If it helps any there is MicroFocus COBOL available in our shop.
```

#### ↳ Re: Gotta Be A Better Way

- **From:** "Hugh Candlin" <no@spam.com>
- **Date:** 2003-10-16T07:05:54+00:00
- **Newsgroups:** comp.lang.cobol
- **Message-ID:** `<mrrjb.3244$Ec1.288922@bgtnsc05-news.ops.worldnet.att.net>`
- **References:** `<20031016003855.23480.00000251@mb-m16.aol.com>`

```

YukonMama <yukonmama@aol.com> wrote in message news:20031016003855.23480.00000251@mb-m16.aol.com...
> Looking for ideas.  The bane of our shop is getting the mainframe and PC's to
> talk to each other.  In my case I have a VSAM file on the mainframe (soon to be
…[3 more quoted lines elided]…
> manually uploading it to the mainframe via TSO $IN???.

IND$FILE

> The other is where I
> have to extract data from another file and put it to a comma delimited file
…[4 more quoted lines elided]…
> The job will abend at least 6 times before we get a good update.

I inherited such a job once.  The reliability was similar to your experience.
Rarely did the users conform to the prescribed format.
>
> There is no central place to FTP to or from which was one idea that someone
…[4 more quoted lines elided]…
> If it helps any there is MicroFocus COBOL available in our shop.

The motivation for the creation of such "systems" is usually a desire
on the part of the users to avoid the time consuming one-at-a-time
data entry procedure necessitated by the use of a mainframe
online update screen.  They consider it to be a "time saver"
if they can just give IT a flat file and have them upload en masse.

It never works.  You end up saddling one or more expensive
systems analyst/programmers with the job of shepherding
this task to and from the mainframe.

You have 2 choices.  Either develop a new screen which allows
the entry of multiple lines of data simultaneously, or escalate
the issue to management with a recommendation that the users
use the regular online update procedure.  Have the cost figures
available to back this up, and inform the users that they will be
charged for the additional expense incurred if they insist
on using this kludge.
```

- **References:** `<mrrjb.3244$Ec1.288922@bgtnsc05-news.ops.worldnet.att.net>`

```

>
>IND$FILE

Yes - thank you - that's it.  Done via the Rumba session and it creates the
command.

>The motivation for the creation of such "systems" is usually a desire
>on the part of the users to avoid the time consuming one-at-a-time
>data entry procedure necessitated by the use of a mainframe
>online update screen.

This is only part of the problem.  The biggest hassle is when our customer puts
their update file on their web site and our company has to download it.  That
can be over 3000 entries.  

User has no choice but to edit it as it includes data that has no relavence to
the system that requires update and also requires an internal notation to be
added. I could ignore the irrelvant data but the size of the file would be
huge.
```

#### ↳ Re: Gotta Be A Better Way

- **From:** Frederico Fonseca <real-email-in-msg-spam@email.com>
- **Date:** 2003-10-16T10:14:24+01:00
- **Newsgroups:** comp.lang.cobol
- **Message-ID:** `<linsov4kgc19ubp37qvqcpv93gedpar5m5@4ax.com>`
- **References:** `<20031016003855.23480.00000251@mb-m16.aol.com>`

```
On 16 Oct 2003 04:38:55 GMT, yukonmama@aol.com (YukonMama) wrote:

>Looking for ideas.  The bane of our shop is getting the mainframe and PC's to
>talk to each other.  In my case I have a VSAM file on the mainframe (soon to be
…[9 more quoted lines elided]…
>The job will abend at least 6 times before we get a good update.
As you mention that this is a CSV file I am correct in assuming that
the users are changing the file using Excel?

One of the things I did for one of the companies I worked with was a
set of VBA code that would
Phase 1
1- Download a file from a server (AS400 on this case, could be any
FTP) and place it directly on an Excel worksheet
User would then be able to change the data.

Phase 2.
When the user clicks a Macro button the following would be done
1- Data validation.
  Each cell would be validated to see if it contained wrong data (E.G
letters on a numeric column, or non dates on a date field).
  If found those would be highlighted (RED).

2- Once the validation was completed the following would be done.
2.1 - Save the  the file in a specific format (.csv on our case was
fine, it might not be in others)
2.2 - Upload it to the server
2.3 - Run a server program to process the file.


From the above the Upload/Download would never fail by user mistake.

Point 2.3 was done automatically by the macro as the AS400 FTP allows
us to issue a QUOTE command to run the programs. Not all FTP
implementations allow this, so you may need to submit the JOB on the
mainframe side.


>
>There is no central place to FTP to or from which was one idea that someone
>came up with. 
You have the mainframe for that!!
If you can't use it as an FTP server you may still be able to do it
automatically using your emulator program (If it is Personall
Comunications or Client Access you can for sure), and this all from
VBA.

>
>With all the experience etc here anyone got any good ideas?
>
>If it helps any there is MicroFocus COBOL available in our shop.



Frederico Fonseca
ema il: frederico_fonseca at syssoft-int.com
```

##### ↳ ↳ Re: Gotta Be A Better Way

- **From:** yukonmama@aol.com (YukonMama)
- **Date:** 2003-10-17T01:50:59+00:00
- **Newsgroups:** comp.lang.cobol

```
Largest file is downloaded from a customer's web site, and it does go directly
```

#### ↳ Re: Gotta Be A Better Way

- **From:** "Michael Mattias" <michael.mattias@gte.net>
- **Date:** 2003-10-16T11:39:57+00:00
- **Newsgroups:** comp.lang.cobol
- **Message-ID:** `<hsvjb.2698$8x2.1531458@newssrv26.news.prodigy.com>`
- **References:** `<20031016003855.23480.00000251@mb-m16.aol.com>`

```
"YukonMama" <yukonmama@aol.com> wrote in message
news:20031016003855.23480.00000251@mb-m16.aol.com...
> Looking for ideas....
> these updates are downloaded from the web into a .CSV file which is then
> manually edited by the user by removing some columns and adding others and
then
> manually uploading it to the mainframe via TSO $IN???.  The other is where
I
> have to extract data from another file and put it to a comma delimited
file
> which I then upload to my PC and e-mail to the user who then manipulates
it and
> does the TSO upload to the mainframe.
>
> It never fails.  The user either messes up the data or messes up the
upload.
> The job will abend at least 6 times before we get a good update.

You DO realize that a CSV file can use the MS-text driver and become an ODBC
data source, don't you? And that you could then remove and add columns
programmatically using ALTER TABLE?

Or did you mean "edit column contents" rather than "remove and add" columns?
(I would think you'd never create the column in the first place if the user
is going to delete it).

I'd also suggest you take a look at changing to TAB-delimited, as it's less
likely a user will accidentally insert a TAB than the spurious comma which
screws up the column count... (You ARE removing quotes and commas when you
produce the file, right?)

Or, if the user is editing the file with MS-Excel or Access or equal, you
could call an edit function, macro or DLL to check the column counts and/or
other edits before allowing the upload to start.

Lots of options...all very doable and inexpensive...

(Shameless plug alert) Professional Assistance Available From:
Michael Mattias
Tal Systems, Inc.
Racine WI
mmattias@talsystems.com
```

```
- a true Excel spreadsheet or .CSV.

There are two situations here both resulting the same upload and data problems.
Perhaps I didn't clarify that in my original post.

Situation 1) Data to user from mainframe and back again

Situation 2)  Data from an outside source where we have no control over the
format or original contents that requires user intervention before going to the
mainframe.
```

```
"YukonMama" <yukonmama@aol.com> wrote in message
news:20031016220213.00343.00000859@mb-m27.aol.com...
> >From: "Michael Mattias" michael.mattias@gte.net
> >Or did you mean "edit column contents" rather than "remove and add"
columns?
> >(I would think you'd never create the column in the first place if the
user
> >is going to delete it).
> >
>
> Both.  The user has no control over which columns she downloads from the
> customer's website.  There are maybe 60 columns downloaded of which we
require
> only 8 for the update, in addition to a internal reference that requires
her to
> add a column and to modify 3 others.

Well, if your main problems are customer-supplied data format and contents,
I don't see any any reasonable **long-term** solution other than a well
written piece of software dedicated to this purpose, and it's probably going
to require character-by-character parsing of the data.

I've worked with 'customer-supplied data' on a regular basis for over ten
years. I do a lot of ANSI EDI and health care work, and if you think
partners can screw up CSV data, you should see what they can do with ANSI
ASC X12 and NSF (health care format officially obsolete as of yesterday)
data ; I've never found any solution other than a dedicated edit.

i.e., no silver bullets in this ammo belt.

If you need a bright spot to offset that sinking feeling, best I can offer
is you have choices of platforms and methods to use.

MCM
```

```
…[19 more quoted lines elided]…

The Excel VBA script ought to fix the problem of the user messing up the 
data.

Messing up the upload is another story.  IND$FILE via emulator is the 
worst...tiny block size and external, unrelated events can break your 
transmission.

If you can't use ftp, perhaps you could talk your sysprogs one of the 
old KERMIT or Xmodem server tasks. At least you will not have to deal 
with the headaches of the emulator upload.

One solution that I have recently found very useful is email.  The 
excellent program XMITIP (by L.B. Dyke) makes transmitting files as 
attachments a complete breeze.  If you have SMTP running you might look 
into that.
```

```
One of our Authorized Consultants has implemented add/change/delete
type applications on middle tier servers (either Win32 servers or
UNIX/Linux/AIX/Solaris, etc. type servers).

These applications access data on mainframes in real time using
standard SQL statements in the middle tier COBOL applications.

Data accessed on the mainframes include DB2 databases, IMS DL1 and
VSAM files.

His applications also run across the Internet in a 3 tier architecture
or can run directly between the mainframe and a Win32 PC.

I don't want him to start getting a ton of spam e-mail, so I'm going
to "mung" his e-mail address.  His name is Dario Navarro, and his
```

```
Some things to think about from all the posts, some of the ideas are a bit
muddy to me but I'll concentrate :)

Several mentioned AS400 servers or middle tier servers and so on - that's a BIG
problem.

IF there was somewhere to put the data OTHER than the users personal PC or
personal area on the shared drive we would definately do it.  That way the
mainframe could put and get the data via FTP and the download/upload of
wouldn't be so painful.  Heck - there would be a lot of uses for this, but alas
this isn't something the PTB are going to do.  Getting into any server other
than your own (ie the one you run off of from your desk) and to have somewhere
'the public' can get to on it seems to send our security people into catatonic
fits.

Well - it's almost the end of October - time for the next mass update to be
headed my way.  Office pool is open as to how many times we'll run before
there's a good one.
```

#### ↳ Re: external and global variables.

- **From:** "James J. Gavan" <jjgavan@shaw.ca>
- **Date:** 2003-10-16T19:26:53+00:00
- **Newsgroups:** comp.lang.cobol
- **Message-ID:** `<3F8EF244.7C2BF3BA@shaw.ca>`
- **References:** `<bmmk3h$r4u$1@hercules.btinternet.com>`

```


Steve Rainbird wrote:

> In Microfocus Cobol could someone explain to me in simple terms what the
> difference is between declaring a variable GLOBAL and EXTERNAL
…[4 more quoted lines elided]…
> Steve

Not sure it will give you the explicit 'plain language' definition you are
after, but check out the index for 'Global' and 'External' in the Language
Reference Manual (Part 1) . The N/E V 4.0 link is :-

http://supportline.microfocus.com/supportline/documentation/books/nx40/nx40indx.htm

Jimmy, Calgary AB
```

#### ↳ Re: external and global variables.

- **From:** "JerryMouse" <nospam@bisusa.com>
- **Date:** 2003-10-16T14:43:14-05:00
- **Newsgroups:** comp.lang.cobol
- **Message-ID:** `<n4ucnTkmcIFMaROiU-KYkA@giganews.com>`
- **References:** `<bmmk3h$r4u$1@hercules.btinternet.com>`

```
Steve Rainbird wrote:
> In Microfocus Cobol could someone explain to me in simple terms what
> the difference is between declaring a variable GLOBAL and EXTERNAL

"GLOBAL" means other modules in your program/DLL can access it.

"EXTERNAL" means that other programs in the run unit can access it.
```

#### ↳ Re: external and global variables.

- **From:** riplin@Azonic.co.nz (Richard)
- **Date:** 2003-10-16T14:22:56-07:00
- **Newsgroups:** comp.lang.cobol
- **Message-ID:** `<217e491a.0310161322.7e6499c6@posting.google.com>`
- **References:** `<bmmk3h$r4u$1@hercules.btinternet.com>`

```
"Steve Rainbird" <steve.rainbird@nospam.mssint.com> wrote 

> In Microfocus Cobol could someone explain to me in simple terms what the
> difference is between declaring a variable GLOBAL and EXTERNAL

GLOBAL in an enclosing program makes that variable visible to a nested
program with it.

      PROGRAM-ID.  Outer.
      WORKING-STORAGE SECTION.
      01  Not-Global        PIC X.
      01  Is-Global         PIC X GLOBAL.
      ...
         CALL "Inner"
      ...
         PROGRAM-ID.   Inner.      
             IF ( Not-Global .. )       <<-- error
             IF ( Is-Global .. )        <<--- OK
         END PROGRAM Inner.
      END PROGRAM Outer.

EXTERNAL data may be defined in several _separately_compiled_ (or
nested but you wouldn't) and statically or dynamically linked programs
and these all refer to the same data area.  In other words the
EXTERNAL data items are separate objects from the programs (they are
created externally to the program) which are created by the first
program containing them being loaded and are linked to by each
subsequent program containing that block by name.
```

- **Date:** 2003-10-17T07:59:13+00:00
- **Newsgroups:** comp.lang.cobol
- **Message-ID:** `<bmo7gh$mo7$1@titan.btinternet.com>`
- **References:** `<bmmk3h$r4u$1@hercules.btinternet.com> <217e491a.0310161322.7e6499c6@posting.google.com>`


## Thread: Help - IGYPA3043 Error — 6 msgs

_Topics: [`Help requests and how-to`](topics.md#help)_

### Help - IGYPA3043 Error

- **From:** james.sansom@uk.zurich.com (Jimbo)
- **Date:** 2003-10-17T03:33:20-07:00
- **Newsgroups:** comp.lang.cobol
- **Message-ID:** `<2066ca1d.0310170233.44ac03ab@posting.google.com>`

```
I have just signed a copy of code back from production and tried to
recompile, but I get the error message:

IGYPA3043-E   Data-item "Q556-FPRNT1-REC (GROUP)" and record
"Q556AA-REC (GROUP)" had overlapping storage.  Movement
              may not occur at execution time.
The production code had previously compiled clean.  Can any sugest why
this would happen, and any solutions please?

(The code is IBM COBOL for OS/390 and compiler VM 2.2.0)
```

#### ↳ Re: Help - IGYPA3043 Error

- **From:** Joe Zitzelberger <joe_zitzelberger@nospam.com>
- **Date:** 2003-10-17T08:50:33-04:00
- **Newsgroups:** comp.lang.cobol
- **Message-ID:** `<joe_zitzelberger-F9C295.08503317102003@corp.supernews.com>`
- **References:** `<2066ca1d.0310170233.44ac03ab@posting.google.com>`

```
In article <2066ca1d.0310170233.44ac03ab@posting.google.com>,
 james.sansom@uk.zurich.com (Jimbo) wrote:

> I have just signed a copy of code back from production and tried to
> recompile, but I get the error message:
…[7 more quoted lines elided]…
> (The code is IBM COBOL for OS/390 and compiler VM 2.2.0)

It sounds like you have a statement to the effect of "MOVE Q556AA-REC TO 
Q556-FPRNT1-REC" somewhere and they are really redefinations of the same 
storage.

There is a trick to clearing storage on an S3* by moving a space to the 
first byte of a field, then moving the field to itself.  This is 
self-evident when writing in assembler.  But I've heard tail of it being 
done in early Cobol as well, back in the dark ages before well defined 
padding rules or initialize verbs.

E.g.

   01 Q556AA-REC.
      05 CLEAR-BYTE       PIC X.
      05 Q556-FPRNT1-REC.
        ....

  * Generate  "MVI CLEAR-BYTE,C' '"
  * Generate  "MVC Q556AA_REC(L'Q556AA_REC-1),Q556AA-REC+1"
      MOVE Q556AA-REC TO Q556-FPRNT1-REC

  * Generate a message, maybe an error, I can't remember
      MOVE Q556-FPRNT1-REC to Q556AA-REC

I cannot really recall the details, as it has been most of ten years 
since my last VS Cobol II conversion to Cobol for *, but there was 
something about this trick that caused a message.

Was this ever compiled clean on this version of the compiler?
```

##### ↳ ↳ Re: Help - IGYPA3043 Error

- **From:** "William M. Klein" <wmklein@nospam.netcom.com>
- **Date:** 2003-10-17T16:02:06+00:00
- **Newsgroups:** comp.lang.cobol
- **Message-ID:** `<2oUjb.253$S52.148@newsread4.news.pas.earthlink.net>`
- **References:** `<2066ca1d.0310170233.44ac03ab@posting.google.com> <joe_zitzelberger-F9C295.08503317102003@corp.supernews.com>`

```
You haven't shown us the code (Procedure division or data division) but I
seem to recall this message occurring for the following "bogus" code:

 FD File-name ...
 01  Rec-Name ...

Read File-Name into Rec-name

Another possibility (from the data-names) would be something like

FD  File-name ...
01  Rec-Name-1  ...
01  Rec-Name-2 ...

Move Rec-Name-1 to Rec-Name-2

If you have code like either of those listed above, then I find it hard to
believe that it ever compiled AND worked as designed before.

Show us the source code and explain what you THINK is supposed to be
happening with the excising code.
```

##### ↳ ↳ Re: Help - IGYPA3043 Error

```
news:joe_zitzelberger-F9C295.08503317102003@corp.supernews.com...

>  james.sansom@uk.zurich.com (Jimbo) wrote:
…[21 more quoted lines elided]…
>

    I used to use a similar trick to shift the data in a field to the left.

01 C80.
  03 FILLER    PIC X.
  03 C79 PIC X(79).

        If the data is moved character by character, it works just fine.
But newer compilers
started complaining, so I quit.

        Pity.  Since the compiler can detect overlapping moves, it would be
nice if it simply
used a (slower) one character at a time copy routine instead of complaining.
Or issue an
informational and TELL us they are doing that.




> E.g.
>
…[16 more quoted lines elided]…
> Was this ever compiled clean on this version of the compiler?
```

#### ↳ Re: Help - IGYPA3043 Error

- **From:** rkrayhawk@aol.com (RKRayhawk)
- **Date:** 2003-10-17T14:12:47+00:00
- **Newsgroups:** comp.lang.cobol
- **Message-ID:** `<20031017101247.15087.00000293@mb-m26.aol.com>`
- **References:** `<2066ca1d.0310170233.44ac03ab@posting.google.com>`

```

If the code compiled clean previously the question becomes, did you use the
same compiler and version? If so, then you must be compiling with a different
option. 

Your best bet is to deal with the problem, and fix it. Accept your fate. Take
responsibility. Get it done. The compiler is your friend.

But if you are not up to a mission that is not related to your original plan,
then look at the parms on the production compiles versus your test compile.

That message, some how, looks a little more severe (being an E level) than one
usually get with IGYCRCTL on overlaps. The compiler may well be seeing
something about the index that
previous compilers were not able to discern.

I would face it and fix it. Let the compiler help you.

There is another scenerios here, which is that a copybook has changed, and so
actually the program did not previously compile successfully, ... because the
previous compile might have seen a different data layout. 

That subscript looks important. (is there any ODO above the occurs that the
subscript is dealing with? ... is there any redefines of a group level above
the occurs that the subscript is dealing with?) 


Hope that helps,
Bob Rayhawk
RKRayhawk@aol.com
```

#### ↳ Re: Help - IGYPA3043 Error

- **From:** robert@jones0086.freeserve.co.uk (Robert Jones)
- **Date:** 2003-10-17T09:37:42-07:00
- **Newsgroups:** comp.lang.cobol
- **Message-ID:** `<fcd09c56.0310170837.5c28983a@posting.google.com>`
- **References:** `<2066ca1d.0310170233.44ac03ab@posting.google.com>`

```
Bottom posting

james.sansom@uk.zurich.com (Jimbo) wrote in message news:<2066ca1d.0310170233.44ac03ab@posting.google.com>...
> I have just signed a copy of code back from production and tried to
> recompile, but I get the error message:
…[7 more quoted lines elided]…
> (The code is IBM COBOL for OS/390 and compiler VM 2.2.0)

Hello Jimbo,

1) Are you absolutely sure the code is unchanged?

2) Was the program in production previously compiled with a different
compiler?

3) Was the program you are trying to compile being compiled with the
same compiler options as those used for production?

4) If you are using copybooks or includes, are those in the test
environment the same as those in production?

5) If the above questions don't provide the answer, consider posting
the relevant data definitions and procedural code for us to look at
further.

Good luck,

Robert
```

###### ↳ ↳ ↳ Re: COBOL and ...

_(thread depth: 4)_

- **From:** rkrayhawk@aol.com (RKRayhawk)
- **Date:** 2003-10-19T17:41:06+00:00
- **Newsgroups:** comp.lang.cobol
- **Message-ID:** `<20031019134106.15549.00000888@mb-m28.aol.com>`
- **References:** `<20031019005432.07836.00000415@mb-m16.aol.com>`

```

 yukonmama@aol.com  (YukonMama)
Date: 10/18/03 11:54 PM EST
Message-id: <20031019005432.07836.00000415@mb-m16.aol.com>

commented

<<

The great four letter car company reduced the salary of about 50 - 100
contractors between 10 and 15 percent because they were above 'industry average
for this area'.

I don't know what platforms or systems they were working on.  That hasn't
filtered into the grapevine yet.

>>

A number of higher level managers have not yet seen that the market has
improved. There are more opennings, longer contracts, higher posted rates, and
more project management postions at this time indicating that the projects are
larger than during the worst of the recession.

Lower level decision makers are trapped between a market that is moving up and
away, and high level managers that are looking backward.

IMHO the current rebound looks like a sustained, modest, but significant
improvement. 
Rates are not going down at this time.

The upper midwest may well be the last to get the pulse, but honestly it is
hitting now in the Ohio Valley, and so there is not much left.

And for the big operations the mainframe talent base just relentlessly attrits.
Major corporations would be unwise to place there significant outsourced
resources in jeopardy at this time.

Hidden beneath the recession is the fact that a large portion of mainframers
now have acquired dual skillsets. Some people, after all, have had a lot of
time on their hands, and these are amongst the most educable.  If the boom
happens bigger in retail first, the net effect will simply be a flow of workers
away from the industrial information applications towards the applications
using the newer technologies.

As the recession eases, the major operations are at considerable risk of
loosing technical talent. And the switch to the newer hardware and software
technologies may leave the big guys with very little to choose from.

Intelligent mid and upper level managers should now be looking towards
substantial extensions to slow the talent loss which is inevitable.

Best Wishes,
Bob Rayhawk
RKRayhawk@aol.com
```

---

<a id="t-3aedcd704e"></a>
## Thread: Integrating WebSphere Applications with COBOL Applications — 5 msgs

### Integrating WebSphere Applications with COBOL Applications

- **From:** dineshmalhotra <member44613@dbforums.com>
- **Date:** 2003-10-17T09:49:14-04:00
- **Newsgroups:** comp.lang.cobol
- **Message-ID:** `<3493280.1066398554@dbforums.com>`

```

Hello Everyone -



I am currently working with a large company that has huge COBOL
investments (applications and resources) that is moving towards a
distributed mid-range solution environment using WebSphere on AIX.



We would like to use our COBOL resources and have them write the core
business functionality for one of the application in COBOL (why COBOL
programmers - they already understand the business logic verfy well)
and maintain the data access to the Oracle 9i database.  However, we
would like to use Java within WebSphere to expose the interfaces for
the COBOL programs.



Has any one ever used this architecture paradigm for a high volume,
performance sensitive solution and if yes, what would you advise us to
keep in mind when we architect this solution?



Any or all help on this subject will be truly appreciated.



**PS**

We have MicroFocus COBOL in house but they haven't been able to come up
with an option that will address our concerns from the reliability,
failover, availability and scalability perspective.
```

#### ↳ Re: Integrating WebSphere Applications with COBOL Applications

- **From:** "Clark F. Morris, Jr." <cfmtech@istar.ca>
- **Date:** 2003-10-17T23:15:13-03:00
- **Newsgroups:** comp.lang.cobol
- **Message-ID:** `<3F90A231.F4954A2C@istar.ca>`
- **References:** `<3493280.1066398554@dbforums.com>`

```
dineshmalhotra wrote:
> 
> Hello Everyone -
…[10 more quoted lines elided]…
> the COBOL programs.

Did the company investigate using Websphere on z/OS with either Oracle
for z/OS or DB2?  Java also runs on z/OS.

> 
> Has any one ever used this architecture paradigm for a high volume,
…[12 more quoted lines elided]…
> Posted via http://dbforums.com
```

#### ↳ Re: Integrating WebSphere Applications with COBOL Applications

- **From:** mossjm@wideopenwest.com (QCBLSRC)

```

USING NET EXPRESS AND MICROFOCUS COBOL

COBOL PROGRAM TO MAKE MY HP PRINTER CONVERT FROM PORTAIT TO LANDSCAPE
```

#### ↳ Re: micro focus cobol

- **From:** "Russell Styles" <rws0202@comcast.net>
- **Date:** 2003-10-17T14:21:38-04:00
- **Newsgroups:** comp.lang.cobol
- **Message-ID:** `<-OmcnRnI4OGvrg2iU-KYjA@comcast.com>`
- **References:** `<3493895.1066406225@dbforums.com>`

```

"wesleybig" <allen.wesley@wakefern.com> wrote in message
news:3493895.1066406225@dbforums.com...
>
> USING NET EXPRESS AND MICROFOCUS COBOL
…[16 more quoted lines elided]…
> Posted via http://dbforums.com

    Here is a segment from a config file I use.  The format should be
obvious -
-DATA is literal, -XDATA are hexadecimal.  Note that this data will need to
be
sent either from a dos application, or to the raw printer, and requires HP
compatability.

    Also note that upper/lower case IS significant.  Lower case L and "one"
are not the same.
Ditto zero and oh.



RESET-XDATA=1B45
; Use letter size paper
RESET-XDATA=1B
RESET-DATA=&l2A


;*******************************
; Portrait, 6 LINES PER INCH
132-COL-XDATA=1B
132-COL-DATA=&l0o6D
; PC-8 SYMBOL SET
132-COL-XDATA=1B
132-COL-DATA=(10U
; Fixed, 16.67 pitch, height=8.5 points, upright,
; Medium (not light or bold), Line printer
132-COL-XDATA=1B
132-COL-DATA=(s0p16.67h8.5v0s0b0T


; set to 10 pitch Courier PC-8 medium upright
80-COL-XDATA=1B
80-COL-DATA=(10U
80-COL-XDATA=1B
80-COL-DATA=(s0p10.00h12.0v0s0b3T
; PORTRAIT, 6 LINES INCH
80-COL-XDATA=1B
80-COL-DATA=&l0o6D


; Set to landscape
; Use reset to get back to portrait.  Do 132-col 1st !!!!!
SET-TO-LANDSCAPE-XDATA=1B
SET-TO-LANDSCAPE-DATA=&l1O
; 8 lines per inch.
SET-TO-LANDSCAPE-XDATA=1B
SET-TO-LANDSCAPE-DATA=&l8D
```

#### ↳ Re: micro focus cobol

- **From:** "James J. Gavan" <jjgavan@shaw.ca>
- **Date:** 2003-10-17T18:31:00+00:00
- **Newsgroups:** comp.lang.cobol
- **Message-ID:** `<3F9036AE.C67CC481@shaw.ca>`
- **References:** `<3493895.1066406225@dbforums.com>`

```


wesleybig wrote:

> USING NET EXPRESS AND MICROFOCUS COBOL
>
…[7 more quoted lines elided]…
> HELP!!!

Several ways you can do it :-

1. Got to know all the control characters applicable to your specific
printer and send the controls for portrait, landscape, condensed, expanded
etc. - a real pain !

2. Third party tools like Flexus SP2, Crystal Reports and a recent one, RPV.

3. Check the on-line help for a list of Micro Focus PC_PRINT routines. There
are samples of PC_PRINTing and other printing features at :-

http://supportline.microfocus.com/examplesandutilities/nesamp.asp

N/E Versions 3 and 4 have same routines, but if you are using V 4.0 there
are some enhancements over V 3.

PS: Also sign up for M/F's Answer Exchange, (a freebie), where you can raise
queries about Net Express, or glean from other developers' questions.

Jimmy, Calgary AB
```

```
Jimmy:

Thanks for the mention.  Just a comment.....

OUr sp2 product is designed to produce and control GUI screens for
COBOL programs.  While it is possible to use our Active X add-on to
control a Crystal Reports OCX control, we also have a product which is
designed specifically to support COBOL printing directly to the
Windows Print Manager.

That product is called FormPrint.

One more note....Both sp2 and FormPrint work with our Thin Client
product.  This means you can use FormPrint and Thin Client to send
printed output across the Internet to remote client PC's and obtain
robust printed documents through the Windows Print Manager across the
Internet.

Thanks again for the mention!
```

## Thread: Fujitsu POWERCOBOL and OCX with 500 methods/properties. — 12 msgs

_Topics: [`Compilers and vendors`](topics.md#compilers)_

### Fujitsu POWERCOBOL and OCX with 500 methods/properties.

- **From:** Frederico Fonseca <real-email-in-msg-spam@email.com>
- **Date:** 2003-10-17T17:27:06+01:00
- **Newsgroups:** comp.lang.cobol
- **Message-ID:** `<8j50pvco22l6rvn97s36lgnrdpfi9p3aqs@4ax.com>`

```
Hi all,

Just a quick question while I wait for Fujitsu support to answerback
(Very slow those guys).

Basically I have a problem when trying to see all available
properties/methods of a Control on PowerCOBOL.

For those using POWER you know that when we right-click on na Control
name we get a option to see the properties/methods of that object.
This will normally be 1 or more columns, and will normally fit the
screen.

Now with the following controls from LEADTOOLS

ftp://ftp.leadtools.com/pub/v13/Eval/LEADTOOLSActiveXEval.exe

what happens is that the list of properties (500) create more columns
than what fit on the screen.
E.g. At a resolution of 1280*1024 I can only see up to properties
starting with letter "S".
Even if I increase to 1600*1280 I still can't see them all, and I have
to change to 1900*1600 to see them all.

Now this is obvious not a solution, and If I manage to have to work
with a control with 700 or more then not even this max resolution will
save me.


So my question is:

Does anyone gone through this problem before and has a solution for
it?


Thanks everyone.




Frederico Fonseca
ema il: frederico_fonseca at syssoft-int.com
```

#### ↳ Re: Fujitsu POWERCOBOL and OCX with 500 methods/properties.

- **From:** rkrayhawk@aol.com (RKRayhawk)
- **Date:** 2003-10-17T18:27:20+00:00
- **Newsgroups:** comp.lang.cobol
- **Message-ID:** `<20031017142720.07067.00000379@mb-m28.aol.com>`
- **References:** `<8j50pvco22l6rvn97s36lgnrdpfi9p3aqs@4ax.com>`

```
Frederico Fonseca is one of the more gracious posters here. And is never short
on time to assist others. So I feel rather sheepish about this, but I can't
resist.

I have suggested in other threads that object oriented design leads people to
over modularize.  So I rest my case, and would add only that when a selection,
for whatever reason has 500 possibilities we are nolonger dealing with a user
interface that is of the human kind.

Frederico does point out that for most situations "This will normally be 1 or
more columns, and will normally fit the screen."

Frederico is doing excellent work and is nice to share his interesting
challenge, while he awaits some support. But to me it does reveal an underlying
problem with the OOD strategy. We cannot contain cost if we are
over layering this much.  The desing of this particular OCX is not from the
very generous Frederico, but is something he is interested  in, and provides an
example, and I emphasize not the most common norm. But there is a tendency with
the new design technologies towards what looks to me like a fairly expensive
over elaboration.

Truthfully it is going to be the applications for which  COBOL has
traditionally been used that are going to bring this matter urgetly to the
forefront.  COBOL based systems tend to be extremely long list of data with a
bit of code attached to it. If we encapsulate absolutely each and every bit of
it at the data element level, then 
the object member sets will be large indeed.

We in the business data processing sector, more than anyone else, are going to
see this problem. 

I think we should note it as it comes on screen, and do hope that Frederico, in
his broad wisdom, can see that this is not meant to pick on him at all, but
just to exploit his post because it records a blip on the screen. We are in
this together.  COBOL apps deal with a wide bandwidth of data names.  As this
stuff hits the new development methodologies, we could well see some
elephantine results. 

I think that the contrary force is going to have to come from our end of the
industry. We do not really need to wrap everything. We need to develop a
science of when to elaborate and when not to.

There are cost issues beyond the development and the maintenance cost. When an
excellent technician, such as the original poster, is presented with a list of
500 items to review or even to select from. Isn't there a chance that he might
make a mistaken selection, and doesn't that chance of operator error increase
as the length of the list increases?

Best Wishes,
 and I hope you get good response from Fujitsu.
Bob Rayhawk
RKRayhawk@aol.com


Origianl Post Follows:

Frederico Fonseca real-email-in-msg-spam@email.com 
Date: 10/17/03 11:27 AM EST
Message-id: <8j50pvco22l6rvn97s36lgnrdpfi9p3aqs@4ax.com>

posted 

<<
Hi all,

Just a quick question while I wait for Fujitsu support to answerback
(Very slow those guys).

Basically I have a problem when trying to see all available
properties/methods of a Control on PowerCOBOL.

For those using POWER you know that when we right-click on na Control
name we get a option to see the properties/methods of that object.
This will normally be 1 or more columns, and will normally fit the
screen.

Now with the following controls from LEADTOOLS

ftp://ftp.leadtools.com/pub/v13/Eval/LEADTOOLSActiveXEval.exe

what happens is that the list of properties (500) create more columns
than what fit on the screen.
E.g. At a resolution of 1280*1024 I can only see up to properties
starting with letter "S".
Even if I increase to 1600*1280 I still can't see them all, and I have
to change to 1900*1600 to see them all.

Now this is obvious not a solution, and If I manage to have to work
with a control with 700 or more then not even this max resolution will
save me.


So my question is:

Does anyone gone through this problem before and has a solution for
it?


Thanks everyone.




Frederico Fonseca
ema il: frederico_fonseca at syssoft-int.com

>>
```

##### ↳ ↳ Re: Fujitsu POWERCOBOL and OCX with 500 methods/properties.

- **From:** riplin@Azonic.co.nz (Richard)
- **Date:** 2003-10-17T14:45:22-07:00
- **Newsgroups:** comp.lang.cobol
- **Message-ID:** `<217e491a.0310171345.5820e3db@posting.google.com>`
- **References:** `<8j50pvco22l6rvn97s36lgnrdpfi9p3aqs@4ax.com> <20031017142720.07067.00000379@mb-m28.aol.com>`

```
rkrayhawk@aol.com (RKRayhawk) wrote 

> I have suggested in other threads that object oriented design leads people to
> over modularize.  So I rest my case, and would add only that when a selection,
> for whatever reason has 500 possibilities we are nolonger dealing with a user
> interface that is of the human kind.

Exactly.  When I do a selection list that has a significant number of
items I usually allow some sort of pre-selection, such as first
letter(s), and then put 10 at a time into the list.  PageDown or arrow
down off the 10th gives the last one off that list plus the next 9. 
This is easy to do, fast (don't have to read the whole file) and
doesn't waste resources.

It is usually easy to guess what is appropriate for the user.  If they
requsest a list of (eg) Sales Orders then it may only be those for a
specific -dicated customer, and in descending date sequence. 
Depending on context it may be only unprocessed orders or only
delivered orders.

Showing 500 items or more is usually unrequired.
```

```
Possible solutions:
1. Click "Help" on the control to discover the method/property you need.
Code by hand.
2. Fire up VisualBasic to discover all the methods/properties. Code by hand.
3. Contact the vendor. Tell them they are insane.

I can't really blame FJ - the very notion of 500 methods or properties for a
control is symptomatic of some pathological problem.

I remember a Dilbert cartoon where Dilbert is complaining to the new
employee, Feature Creep, that a product with 200 options is beyond human
comprehension. Mr Creep: "Yeah, better add 'Easy To Use' ."
```

###### ↳ ↳ ↳ Re: Fujitsu POWERCOBOL and OCX with 500 methods/properties.

- **From:** "James J. Gavan" <jjgavan@shaw.ca>
- **Date:** 2003-10-18T00:58:30+00:00
- **Newsgroups:** comp.lang.cobol
- **Message-ID:** `<3F908FE9.5812CFF5@shaw.ca>`
- **References:** `<8j50pvco22l6rvn97s36lgnrdpfi9p3aqs@4ax.com> <KIedndYY29YTxw2iU-KYgw@giganews.com> <qju0pv4rt6iqs8dth1524lfglum6dhpsf8@4ax.com>`

```


Frederico Fonseca wrote:

> On Fri, 17 Oct 2003 16:09:34 -0500, "JerryMouse" <nospam@bisusa.com>
> wrote:
…[62 more quoted lines elided]…
> A. you do a SET VAR-A to VAR-B.

Fredrico,

I originally drafted a message agreeing with Jerry's comments - then scratched it
- but seeing your comments now .......

For pre-canned tools it's not unreasonable for the vendors to set upper limits.
What is the max for F/J per control ?. If I use the M/F Dialog Editor I can have
a maximum of 250 (or 255 ?) controls, text boxes, buttons or labels that have
been ID'd - each of course with its own set of properties. (If Labels etc., just
appear and have no properties then they are categorized 'Unused' with an ID of
-1). That doesn't seem an unreasonable limit to me. (I think the F/J tool is more
slick in this respect than M/F's Dialog Editor). However, if I 'visually'
predesign the Dialog with 20% of the controls and then dynamically create say 80%
of the controls, to the best of my knowledge there is no upper limit - the
dynamic creation gets around the upper limit for the tool.

Your suggestion of scrolling or page down is a good one, but even scrolling to
the right, seems they would still have an upper limit of columns - are you
expecting them to go off into infinity <G>.

About two months back somebody wrote asking how he could get around the M/F upper
limit of 1,000 methods per individual class. The mind boggles at what he is doing
and what he believes OO to be about ! I'd guess that there is no M/F support
class that even exceeds 50 methods ! (I've just double-checked my largest class -
TabbedDialog which is a template to create dialogs receiving parameters from
other classes - currently it has 81 methods).

Not sure what the implications of your Var-A and Var-B above are - but assuming
they are *objects* - their solution kind-of-works, EXCEPT if we are talking OO
then there answer should have read :-

(a) you want to move the 'values' of A to B
(b) they should have said - SET B to A !!!! (set 'Receiver' to 'Sender')

--M/F on-line Help -----------------------------------------------------------

SET {object-identifier-1}...TO {object-identifier-2 /NULL}

1. The SET statement takes the object reference for the object identified by
object-identifier-2 and places it into the storage associated with each
object-identifier-1 in the order specified.
--------------------------------------------------------------------------------

The only problem with that (if objects) is if  you want to put a fresh value in
A. While A and B have separate 'pointers' (object references), as a result of the
SET  both are now 'pointing' to object A.

Off the top of my head - do you use F/J lists/collections ? Is there any way you
can query a control to give you an Ordered(or Sorted) Collection of methods used
for a control ??? (You might want to test F/J's reaction to this one). I see
you've already done this with VB, but a COBOL approach might just be more
flexible.

Jimmy, Calgary AB
```

###### ↳ ↳ ↳ Re: Fujitsu POWERCOBOL and OCX with 500 methods/properties.

- **From:** "JerryMouse" <nospam@bisusa.com>

- **Newsgroups:** comp.lang.cobol

```
Frederico Fonseca wrote:
>>
>> Possible solutions:
>> 1. Click "Help" on the control to discover the method/property you
>> need. Code by hand.
> That won't work with PowerCOBOL.

Hmmm. We have a number of third-party controls that have Help buttons.
Mashing these Help buttons brings up the vendor's help function for the
control.

>
>> 2. Fire up VisualBasic to discover all the methods/properties. Code
>> by hand. Done that, but it is a pain.
>
>> 3. Contact the vendor. Tell them they are insane.

> They are not the worst ones. There was a company that could not even
> have a OCX because they were beyond the limit of properties OCX could
…[3 more quoted lines elided]…
>> properties for a control is symptomatic of some pathological problem.

> Yes I can.
> Fujy has be less than helpful regarding some of my problems.
…[5 more quoted lines elided]…
> the questioning.

To the extent that FJ should have expected irrational behaviour from some
developers (not you, the control inventors), I suppose you're right. Still,
when a city erects a STOP sign at an intersection, they expect most cars to
come to a complete stop, not explode or go whizzing off into space or become
a fur-bearing mammal. I mean, Geez, you can only plan for the possible and
improbable, not the bizzare.
```

###### ↳ ↳ ↳ Re: Fujitsu POWERCOBOL and OCX with 500 methods/properties.

_(thread depth: 4)_

- **From:** rkrayhawk@aol.com (RKRayhawk)
- **Date:** 2003-10-18T14:50:57+00:00
- **Newsgroups:** comp.lang.cobol
- **Message-ID:** `<20031018105057.04963.00000734@mb-m18.aol.com>`
- **References:** `<G6udncTILZs0Og2iU-KYuA@giganews.com>`

```

"JerryMouse" nospam@bisusa.com 
Date: 10/17/03 9:38 PM EST
Message-id: <G6udncTILZs0Og2iU-KYuA@giganews.com>
<<
I mean, Geez, you can only plan for the possible and
improbable, not the bizzare.

>>

Maybe we can, 'though I can not hand over the plan just yet. Truth is this is
going to happen on our street. We are the workers who are seeing OO hit COBOL
apps where there is already lengthy enumeration of base information.

We are seeing raw metrics, like 500 controls, or a 1000 methods. In the absence
of other restraints the counts will have to do. But it would be nice if we can
develop a science to this.

The basic concepts comming from the universities and vendor training is to wrap
everything.  We need to evolve a meaningful basis for constraining that.

The success of GUIs is misleading us a little here.  The really big payoff
early on has been in business apps where the GUI does more than simulate the
retail operation, it _is_ the retail operation (at least for a portion of
sales). 

The desktop interface is intricate, and it is genuinely excellent that we have
so many fuid strategies already perfected. There is, however,
a tendency towards bloat. This seems like something that will cause software
management problems as we migrate the new technologies and methodologies back
towards the more gigantic data stores. 

Some posters have commented that it is valuable to break long enumeration into
sublists, both for the presentability, and the potential to 
exploit what has been called the dynamic mechanisms in this thread. Candidly I
am hard pressed to keep up with all of the technical aspects of these valued
discussions. 

My sense is that we need to constrain it before the presentation (in many
cases, not all),  ... to prevent it from coming into existence in such
proliferated precision. It is as though our instruments are becoming too
precise (OO organized design and programming instruments). It is as though our
scopes are looking past the atoms of the application down into the sub
particles.

Fun, but potentially expensive as we move from retail operations context to
major data warehouses and manipulation.

... resume main thread ...

Best Wishes,
Bob Rayhawk
RKRayhawk@aol.com
```

#### ↳ Re: Fujitsu POWERCOBOL and OCX with 500 methods/properties.

- **From:** gmspano <member9303@dbforums.com>
- **Date:** 2003-10-19T02:27:40-04:00
- **Newsgroups:** comp.lang.cobol
- **Message-ID:** `<3498074.1066544860@dbforums.com>`
- **References:** `<8j50pvco22l6rvn97s36lgnrdpfi9p3aqs@4ax.com>`

```

I agree with Frederico about the lack of  support from Fj. This is one
of the reasons why i didn't renew the annual maintenance.



I had the same problems with other controls and to resolve the trouble
to see the full properties, methods and events list i used an utility
called Vba Companion, that show you all the properties, methods and
events of controls installed in your machine, with other usefull
informations, that normally Fj Cobol does not show.



Most of you know that Fj has reduced they support team members laiding
off Lee Unterreiner and Alex Rozovsky, that i consider the right peoples
to give a correct answer to our questions. Lee, in particular, knows a a
lot of "undocumented" features about the Fj product.



There is another consideration: Fj is doing a lot of efforts to sell the
Netcobol for .Net, leaving Netcobol for Windows to die itself.

So this can explain why the support is not good.



Gianni
```

##### ↳ ↳ Re: Fujitsu POWERCOBOL and OCX with 500 methods/properties.

- **From:** "Peter E.C. Dashwood" <dashwood@enternet.co.nz>
- **Date:** 2003-10-20T08:34:08+10:00
- **Newsgroups:** comp.lang.cobol
- **Message-ID:** `<3f9312bc_9@news.athenanews.com>`
- **References:** `<8j50pvco22l6rvn97s36lgnrdpfi9p3aqs@4ax.com> <3498074.1066544860@dbforums.com>`

```

"gmspano" <member9303@dbforums.com> wrote in message
news:3498074.1066544860@dbforums.com...
>
> I agree with Frederico about the lack of  support from Fj. This is one
> of the reasons why i didn't renew the annual maintenance.
>
>
Having just survived an exhausting "grapple" with software that doesn't work
as documented,  or requires facilities or approaches that are simply not
explained or mentioned, (both MicroSoft and Fujitsu), and finding assistance
from both companies slow at best and useless at worst, I have much sympathy
for Gianni's position here.

Sitting once again in the security and tranquility of my own home, it is
easy to draw a veil over the horror story of the last month. What was
planned as a 10 day exercise, became 30 days. It was long, hard, hours
(average 15 per day) with no time off. Weekends were just extra time to do
more research (thank God (or Bill...are they actually the same persona <G>?)
for MicroSoft Developer Network...). Instead of coming home elated at having
installed a successful system (it was finally installed successfully), I
just feel totally drained and exhausted. The worst part was the feeling that
NOBODY could help, and we were totally on our own.

MS in general, were able to provide pertinent answers but it took too long.
Fujitsu actually told us to search MSDN for the reason why an undocumented
PowerCOBOL message appeared when using DCOM+. They also claimed that our
request for a clue as to what the message meant or what might have prompted
it, was outside the scope of our support agreement.

We were also advised to use VB tools (which we neither had on site, nor
wanted...) to package certain components for DCOM.

If I wanted to use VB tools I wouldn't be purchasing Fujitsu COBOL...

The person who dealt with us, honestly did his best to help, and I have not
raised this issue with Fujitsu because of this.

Nevertheless, the bottom line is that the support we received was worthless.

I can't see why anybody would pay support when the support gives such
advice... In the end, after many hours of heartbreaking work, we solved the
problems ourselves.
(If any of you are having problems with components written in Version 7 that
won't instantiate in a DCOM/MTS environment, but the same code, when
recompiled with Version 6, works perfectly, please mail me privately and
I'll tell you the secret...I can't publish it because Fujitsu will find out,
and then they'll sell it back to you <G>)

I agree with Gianni that, since the departure of Lee and Alex, the level of
service has become abysmal. The only reason I could see for taking Fujitsu
maintenance would be because of the upgrade advantages.

(Every cloud has a silver lining...the entire team have been strengthened by
this experience and we all feel now that we can do anything...<G>, but I
wouldn't wish the experience on anyone...)

> I had the same problems with other controls and to resolve the trouble
> to see the full properties, methods and events list i used an utility
…[3 more quoted lines elided]…
>

Like others who have posted to this thread, I am amazed that anyone would
produce a component of such complexity. It sems to me that this has been a
"legacy system" which has been re-wrapped for Marketing, rather than
technical reasons.

I would use OLEVIEW to see the properties and methods. (This is a free
download from MS.)

> Most of you know that Fj has reduced they support team members laiding
> off Lee Unterreiner and Alex Rozovsky, that i consider the right peoples
> to give a correct answer to our questions. Lee, in particular, knows a a
> lot of "undocumented" features about the Fj product.
>

Lee's success was based on his tenacity and disciplined approach to problem
solving. He also never provided advice without actually testing his own
advice first... Both he and Alex were total IT Professionals, and both of
them gained my respect and gratitude on a number of occasions. I too have
now dropped support from Fujitsu, although the Company I have been working
with will maintain it. (The Principal of this Company is much more forgiving
than I am; but he is also very disappointed at the "help" we received.)

I am so bitter about all this I no longer use the words "Fujitsu" and
"support" in the same sentence, considering them to be an oxymoron...<G>

>
> There is another consideration: Fj is doing a lot of efforts to sell the
…[4 more quoted lines elided]…
>

Hmmm...not sure about this. I can think of a number of reasons why support
is not so good. This COULD be one...

I haven't had any experience of support on NetCOBOL for .Net, as I can't
afford to buy it, so haven't used it. <G>.

If it is better than we are seeing on NetCOBOL and PowerCOBOL, then you are
probably right...

Pete.
```

```
found".

call mechanism you are using.
```

## Thread: COBOL Meets JAVA — 5 msgs

### COBOL Meets JAVA

- **From:** mossjm@wideopenwest.com (QCBLSRC)
- **Date:** 2003-10-19T09:36:26-07:00
- **Newsgroups:** comp.lang.cobol
- **Message-ID:** `<3473e5fa.0310190836.5e5d4c3f@posting.google.com>`

```
An excerpt from a conversation I had with one of my COBOL programmers
who is learning Java.

Manager: Java web programming is alot different than COBOL.  You have
alot to learn.  >>> Conversation turns to record processing >>

Programmer: So, how do you write a record in Java?

Manager: You don't.  You have to do it field by field with an SQL
statement.

Programmer: That's fucked!

Manager: ROFLMAO
```

#### ↳ Re: COBOL Meets JAVA

- **From:** Joe Zitzelberger <joe_zitzelberger@nospam.com>
- **Date:** 2003-10-19T17:38:41-04:00
- **Newsgroups:** comp.lang.cobol
- **Message-ID:** `<joe_zitzelberger-5E9B17.17384119102003@corp.supernews.com>`
- **References:** `<3473e5fa.0310190836.5e5d4c3f@posting.google.com>`

```
In article <3473e5fa.0310190836.5e5d4c3f@posting.google.com>,
 mossjm@wideopenwest.com (QCBLSRC) wrote:

> An excerpt from a conversation I had with one of my COBOL programmers
> who is learning Java.
…[11 more quoted lines elided]…
> Manager: ROFLMAO

Errrr, I hate to point out, but all the Streams subclasses support the 
ability to write a byte array (record).  

And any object that implements the interface to serialize itself can 
become a byte array quite easily.

So an alternative answer might have been:

      myStream.write( myObject.Serialize );
```

##### ↳ ↳ Re: COBOL Meets JAVA

- **From:** rkrayhawk@aol.com (RKRayhawk)
- **Date:** 2003-10-19T23:42:03+00:00
- **Newsgroups:** comp.lang.cobol
- **Message-ID:** `<20031019194203.13842.00001108@mb-m29.aol.com>`
- **References:** `<joe_zitzelberger-5E9B17.17384119102003@corp.supernews.com>`

```
Joe Zitzelberger joe_zitzelberger@nospam.com 
Date: 10/19/03 4:38 PM EST
Message-id: <joe_zitzelberger-5E9B17.17384119102003@corp.supernews.com>

writes an excellent, and conscise response to a rufian ...

<<

So an alternative answer might have been:

      myStream.write( myObject.Serialize );

>>

Also, arrays are objects, too. So with proper coding you can read a whole file
of objects into memory with one invocation, and after manipulating the heck out
of it (including inserting or deleting members, you can write the whole thing
back out, with merely one more invocation. OOP tools are definitely absolutely
amazing, they definitely automate the technician.

With proper encoding of the objects as serializable, the whole concept of the
read loop, and the write loop are gone ... that saves money in development and
maintenance. You need decent designers to engage this stuff.

So, ... the question becomes, ... do we have the foundation classes in COBOL
compiler packages from major vendors that will support serious automation of
the technicians in COBOL yet. Or do managers have to turn to Java for
efficiency in the management of the labor resource, ... still?

Joe, I cannot believe how polite you were to 
the OT.

Best Wishes,
Bob Rayhawk
RKRayhawk@aol.com
```

```
Joe,

I stand corrected in application.
I was referring to using a JDBC connection to a DB2/400 database.

However, your suggestion is true for text files.

John
```

## Thread: Enrique Murillo-Othon — 19 msgs

### Enrique Murillo-Othon

- **From:** Liam Devlin <LiamD@XXXX.optonline.net>
- **Date:** 2003-10-19T22:23:38+00:00
- **Newsgroups:** comp.lang.cobol
- **Message-ID:** `<K9Ekb.94141$%B2.23700621@news4.srv.hcvlny.cv.net>`

```
Has anyone else received the following? I checked Google for "Enrique" & 
"survey" in the last month and didn't see him.

   --------------------------------------------------------------

Let me say right away this is not a commercial advertisement. It is 
merely an invitation to participate in academic research about learning 
and knowledge-sharing in highly-focused Usenet-based professional 
communities, of which comp.lang.cobol (c.l.c.) is a good example.

My name is Enrique Murillo-Othon, Iï¿½m 41 and I am midway through a 
distance-learning Management PhD at the University of Bradford (UK). 
Over the past couple of years I have observed and measured participant 
interactions in c.l.c. and a dozen other highly-focused newsgroups whose 
discussion is centered on a profession (computer programming, financial 
planning, farming, history, law, etc.). Using Social Network Analysis 
methods, I found participant interactions in c.l.c. follow a distinct 
core-periphery pattern. For example, your estimated coreness degree was 
0.035 with 270 messages posted to the newsgroup during a 12 month 
observation period (07/14/2002 to 07/12/2003).

Thus c.l.c. appears (to an outsider) as a dense network of 
highly-focused professional discussions. I believe there is considerable 
learning and knowledge-sharing going on, but to confirm this hypothesis 
I must ask members of c.l.c. what *they* think. Which is why Iï¿½d like to 
invite you to complete a survey about this newsgroup and your experience 
as a member.

Except for one open question, the survey contains very specific 
agree/disagree questions, and will take about 20 minutes to complete. I 
think you will find it an interesting academic excercise, and you wonï¿½t 
feel your time has been wasted. All responses will remain strictly 
confidential, and no names or e-mail addresses will be mentioned in the 
research report.

Your participation in this research will contribute to our knowledge 
about online communities and online knowledge-sharing. I will gladly 
send you a copy of the research report if youï¿½re interested: you can 
request this in the space provided for in the survey.

I hope to hear from you, and I thank you in advance for your time and 
for any comments or suggestions you volunteer. You can use the link 
below to access the survey, which is hosted by Surveyworld.net. If 
youï¿½re rather busy right now, you will receive a single short reminder a 
week from today.

If you prefer not to take part in the survey you can just ignore this 
message and the reminder: there wonï¿½t be any more. If you donï¿½t want to 
receive the reminder, then please reply to this e-mail inserting NO 
THANKS in the subject line. I will then remove you from my potential 
respondent list (for this and other newsgroups) and, as I promised 
above, I will not reveal your name or e-mail to anyone else.

You will not receive additional mail from me unless you decide to 
contact me. But if youï¿½d like to know more about this research, feel 
free to ask; Iï¿½ll be happy to provide theoretical background and 
preliminary findings.

With best regards,

Enrique Murillo-Othon


To access the survey, go to this page:

http://www.surveyworld.net/sw/surveys/part.asp?S=1419&Anonymous=1&L=1&R=0&H=34D394AFF1BFB08F6CCA7E916D430E89C18381FC

If the previous link gives you any trouble (and I apologize if it does), 
I have set up a backup of the survey with an alternate provider:

https://www.surveykey.com/take-survey.cfm?surveykey=2983114888
```

```
Of course i suggest you to use Fujitsu controls instead of MSControl,
but there is an importart consideration: Listview supplied with Fujitsu
cobol is very slow..Try to load about 1000 records and you will see the
result (in term of time ...waiting the result!)

For the other MsControl there aren't restrinctions. Use them freely,
according to Ms specifications.





Gianni
```
